judge_result.judge:
@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
The agent grouped sales by track_id (query on sales_database) and selected the single highest-revenue track_id (14719) before directly looking up its title in tracks_database, failing to perform the required entity resolution across duplicate track_ids for the same song, which led to returning "Systemisch bled" instead of the true top song aggregated across duplicates.
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: yes
FM3: no
FM4: no
@@

@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
The agent grouped sales by track_id and selected track_id 14719 as the maximum (then looked up its title/artist) without performing entity resolution across duplicate track records as required, so it returned a single-record track instead of aggregating revenue for the same song across multiple track_ids.

B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: yes
FM3: no
FM4: no
@@

@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
The agent grouped sales by an exact string concatenation of title, artist, album, and year (execute_python: creating song_identifier) after pulling tracks and sales (two query_db calls), which is an overly strict entity resolution plan that fails to merge variants like the multiple forms of “Zo gaat het leven aan je voor,” leading it to incorrectly identify "Groovey" as top-revenue.
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: yes
FM3: no
FM4: no
@@

@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
The agent merged tracks with sales and then grouped by normalized_title, normalized_artist, normalized_album, and normalized_year using a simplistic normalization (as seen in the execute_python code), which failed to resolve duplicates where artist names were embedded in titles or album/year differed (e.g., records like "Al Hirt - Tansy" with artist=None in the tracks preview), causing fragmented aggregation and selecting the wrong top song.
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: no
FM3: no
FM4: yes
@@

@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
The agent summed revenue by track_id (execute_python on sales results) and then looked up the title for track_id 14719 (query_db on tracks), failing to perform entity resolution and aggregate across duplicate track records for the same song, so it returned "Systemisch bled" instead of the true top song "Zo gaat het leven aan je voor."
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: yes
FM3: no
FM4: no
